accepted_findings: The full eight-form, forty-view closeup source family remains byte-structurally unchanged and available for morphology review; the runtime family is a separate artifact rather than a destructive simplification.

One-in-six deterministic far-card selection plus 1.28x card expansion reduces the trunk-plus-far-crown family from 367,918,128 bytes to 20,264,400 bytes in the final run, a 94.492 percent resource reduction.
The runtime family contains 435,064 render vertices and 223,592 render triangles across eight independent forms, versus 6,961,948 vertices and 3,859,140 triangles in the full family.
A UE 5.8 Metal map saves and renders 512 trees through sixteen HISM components with shared trunk/crown ground transforms and no collision.

After correcting the first benchmark's independently grounded crown bug, the retained frame keeps foliage on the branch structure and removes the false forest-floor leaf carpet and bare-crown failure.
rejection_reasons: The 512-tree frame is a flat neutral benchmark, not source-matched Futaleufu terrain, slope, river, light, fog, weather, or seasonal context.

The stand reads as an even-aged plantation with regular spacing and a continuous dark crown wall rather than mapped Patagonian forest bands with natural gaps, disturbance, and aspect/elevation response.
Thirty-five percent of the frame falls below luma 45; transmission, exposure, crown depth, and shadow response need corridor photometry review before this can support a lifelike river frame.
The far representation intentionally omits branchlets, near leaves, understory, saplings, deadwood, epiphytes, shrubs, and the other native species required by the ecology contract.
No near-to-far transition, temporal wind, motion-vector, masked-overdraw, draw-call, streaming, culling, packaged desktop, or on-device VR evidence exists.
The accepted resource reduction does not resolve the V10 closeup junction and projected-silhouette rejection in the full source family.
next_iteration_requirements: Add a deterministic near/mid/far transition test with camera motion and no visible crown pop, branch disappearance, or material change.
Profile the 512-tree map in a packaged desktop build and on the project VR target with warmup, frame-time percentiles, draw calls, overdraw, streaming, culling, and memory capture.
Place the retained far family through source masks, slope, aspect, elevation, riparian setback, disturbance gaps, and non-grid blue-noise ecology in a bounded Futaleufu corridor copy.
Add native understory, sapling, deadwood, and at least one additional reviewed Nothofagus-family crown set before ecology review.
Tune two-sided transmission and corridor photometry while preserving the project-owned material provenance and an engine-measured far-family resource reduction above 94 percent.
